title: 慢下来才能更快：AI 如何重塑软件工程
author: The Pragmatic Engineer
content:
Slow down to speed up: AI and software engineering

Modern software development is undergoing a paradigm shift. With the emergence of highly sophisticated LLMs and context-aware tools like Cursor, Anthropic's Claude, and OpenAI's Codex, engineering teams are pumping out logic faster than ever. However, this unmatched velocity brings severe structural fragmentation, a clear decline in software reliability, and completely transformed internal cultural pressures.

The Real-World Costs of AI Over-Reliance

The rush to fully automate code creation has already induced painful consequences at elite technology firms. At Meta, an unprecedented security vulnerability hit Instagram when an unauthenticated password reset mechanism was made live. This flaw was traced directly to code that was written by AI and reviewed by AI without any human verification steps.

Following this major infrastructure failure, Meta's Chief Information Security Officer unexpectedly resigned during an active critical incident investigation. This development matches a cultural environment where elite software teams are facing massive restructuring, forced data labeling reassignments, and an extreme drop in morale.

Perverse Metrics: The Rise of Token Maxing

When organizations integrate AI statistics directly into software engineer evaluations, they often prompt unexpected behavior. A phenomenon termed token maxing has swept through prominent engineering operations, including Meta and Amazon. Afraid of potential downsizings, engineers began deliberately inflating their automated system volume:

  • Executing arbitrary code instructions simply to boost leaderboard placement.
  • Utilizing AI to parse basic documentation instead of reading it directly.
  • Prioritizing metric-driven system volume over shipping actual, practical product components.

Systemic Outages and the Quality Crisis

This lack of human verification has triggered massive technical fallout at leading infrastructure platforms. Amazon recently suffered a core platform disruption when an internal generative layout engine accidentally cleared out an active production environment. Concurrently, GitHub experienced persistent performance instabilities, operating with diminished nines of availability due to a massive threefold load increase generated by automated development workflows.

Traditional systems are buckling because codebases are expanding far quicker than structural verification layers can keep pace. As industry veterans note, modern development operations are accumulating raw code far faster than they are accumulating system trust.

Pragmatic Strategies for the Modern Developer

To safely traverse this complex landscape, engineers and technical leaders must embrace a philosophy of measured execution:

  1. Cap Tooling Workflows: Limit your daily generative generation to a volume that you can confidently audit, test, or systematically verify.
  2. Clean Technical Debt: Leverage AI to quickly resolve embedded legacy debt, transforming yourself into an architectural maintainer.
  3. Protect System Learning: Avoid treating automated systems as an absolute substitute for deep domain knowledge. If you allow automated tools to dictate implementation details while ignoring underlying abstractions, present speed comes at the cost of long-term professional technical skill.
aiSummary: Gergely Orosz 认为，AI 编程让软件团队显著提速，但真正的瓶颈已经转向验证能力、工程文化、成本控制，以及像积累代码一样快速积累信任的能力。
oneSentenceSummary: Gergely Orosz 认为，AI 编程让软件团队显著提速，但真正的瓶颈已经转向验证能力、工程文化、成本控制，以及像积累代码一样快速积累信任的能力。
summary: 这场演讲以一线观察串起 Meta、Anthropic、OpenAI、Cursor、Google、Uber、创业公司和传统企业中的 AI 编程变化。Orosz 先从一起据称由 AI 编写并由 AI 审查代码导致的 Instagram 安全事故讲起，再延伸到 token maxing、裁员、信任与安全团队调整，以及激励机制整体失衡的问题。他也给出平衡视角：智能体已经真正有用，团队能交付更多代码，大公司正在建设定制化 AI 开发平台，编程模型也越来越成为日常工作的核心。最重要的洞察是，个人提速本身不够，长期收益依赖团队级智能体系统、质量门禁、架构、代码审查、成本纪律和领域经验。演讲给出的建议很务实：把智能体使用量限制在自己能审查或验证的范围内，用 AI 减少技术债，持续学习而不是外包判断，并且慢下来，搭建能安全承载更高速度的系统。

mainPoints.point: AI 编程速度正在超过验证能力
mainPoints.explanation: 演讲反复展示团队借助智能体产出更多代码，但瓶颈已经转移到审查、测试、架构，以及对最终系统的信任。

mainPoints.point: 指标驱动的 AI 采用会制造扭曲激励
mainPoints.explanation: Orosz 描述了大公司中的 token maxing 现象：工程师为了内部排行榜和绩效信号而抬高 AI 使用量，因为系统奖励的是活动量，而不是实际交付价值。

mainPoints.point: 优秀公司会构建系统级 AI 工作流
mainPoints.explanation: Uber、Google、OpenAI 等大型组织不只是购买通用工具，而是把智能体整合进内部代码审查、MCP 网关、代码仓库、实验流程和风险系统。

mainPoints.point: 质量下滑已经在生产软件中显现
mainPoints.explanation: 来自 Anthropic、OpenAI、Amazon、GitHub 和开源工具的案例说明了一个更广泛的担忧：AI 辅助开发可能交付更多临时补丁、脆弱接口和线上故障。

mainPoints.point: 高级判断力和领域经验变得更重要，而不是更不重要
mainPoints.explanation: 演讲认为，AI 会放大有经验工程师的能力，因为他们能定义、验证和引导系统；而初级工程师或非专家如果跳过必要学习，反而更难构建可维护的软件。

keyQuotes: 导致问题的是由 AI 编写、又由 AI 而非人类审查的代码
成熟来自把 AI 应用于系统，而不是应用于个人
我们积累代码的速度，比积累信任的速度更快。
不要外包学习，因为让 AI 写代码、自己跳过所有学习实在太容易了
我的建议是，把你每天使用智能体的量限制在你能够审查或验证的范围内
